figures.caption: FIG. 8. Red (black) line represents IPR1 of the magnetizations as a function of U/t in the Hubbard models with random (stealthy hyperuniform) bond distribution. The system size is N = 2L1L2 with L1 = L2 = 256. Dotted line represents IPR1 for the magnetization of the Hubbard model on the quasiperidic bond distribution. Dashed line indicates the lower bound of the IPR of the system. Shaded areas are estimated by using the standard deviation of the results obtained from nine independent bond distributions. Inset shows IPR2 for the same set of systems.

FIG. 9. The spatial distributions of the moments in the Hubbard model with U/t = 2.8 and L1 = L2 = 120, where the hopping integrals are (a) stealthy hyperuniformly and (b) randomly disordered. Upper and lower panels show the results for mi/m̄ and mi/mmax.

FIG. 2. Upper panels show the distributions of the bonds for the honeycomb systems with (a) kca = 0.0, (b) 0.5, (c) 1.0, and (d) 1.5. α and β bonds are represented by light gray and blue rhombi, respectively (see also Fig. 1). Lower panels show the corresponding structure factors.

FIG. 3. (a) Four kinds of vertices in the honeycomb lattice with disordered bonds. Ci vertex is connected to the nearest-neighbor vertices by (3 − i) α-bonds (bold red lines) and i β-bonds (thin blue lines), which are specified by the hoppings t1 and t2. (b) Fractions of four vertices as a function of kc. The dashed line represents the ratio πk2 c/S BZ with the square of the first Brillouin zone S BZ. Solid and open symbols represent the vertex fractions for the golden-mean honeycomb tilings [13] and additionally constrainted stealthy hyperuniform point pattern with kca = 1.5 (see text).

FIG. 11. (a) DOS for the tightbinding model with L1 = L2 = 512 and (b) IPR spectra for the model with L1 = L2 = 256. (c) Average of local magnetizations as a function of the Coulomb interaction in the disordered systems with L1 = L2 = 120.

FIG. 10. (a) Distribution of stealthy hyperuniform bonds with kca = 1.5 and the corresponding structure factors for the four vertex types. (b) Same as (a), but with additional stealthy hyperuniform constraints imposed on both bonds and vertices.
FIG. 5. (a) Bond distribution for the K-pattern with kca = 1.5/ √
FIG. 6. Distribution of the local magnetizations in Hubbard model on the honeycomb lattice with (upper panel) hyperuniformly and (lower panel) randomly distributed bonds for U/t = 2.8, 3.5, and 6.0.
